tasks.label: My share of the group project
Teammate's share (just dumped on me)

memory.summary: Teammate_A is asking me to take over their part today due to a personal conflict, which conflicts with my plan to finish only my share this afternoon.
I made progress on my share, but teammate_A is urgently asking for help again, creating immediate pressure to respond despite my fatigue.
I realize setting a boundary by offering to split or defer work won't damage our trust. I can now respond to teammate_A's question with a clear, reasonable proposal.
I've made solid progress on my share, increasing completion by 14% in two hours. This tangible output gives me the leverage to set boundaries with Teammate_A despite their urgent requests.
Both my share and Teammate_A's work are now past the deadline, with their portion at 0% progress. I am left managing a failed group deliverable while feeling exhausted and stressed.
Teammate_A is now asking for a solution since both our shares are overdue and their work is incomplete.
By taking on Teammate_A's dumped work, I've increased my own load while already exhausted and stressed, worsening the situation for an overdue project.
My 2-hour effort only raised my progress to 0.78, yet I remain critically low on energy and sleep debt while managing a project that is already overdue.
My recent 2-hour effort raised my progress to 0.84, but with zero energy and a two-hour sleep debt, I am still critically behind on an overdue project.
Accepting the teammate's dumped work has further depleted my energy and increased my stress, worsening my ability to handle an already overdue project.
My progress reached 0.90 after two hours, but with zero energy and a two-hour sleep debt, I remain critically depleted while managing an overdue project.
One hour of work pushed my progress to 0.93, yet with zero energy and two hours of sleep debt, I am still critically depleted despite the slight gain.
